one_time_description: Forgiveness of Treaty loan (land claim negotiation debt) owed to Canada by Ahousaht, effective March 31, 2020
economic_notes: Nation is a member of Ma-Mook Development Corporation (20% interest), a five-nation forestry organization not consolidated. Major capital projects underway: Wastewater Treatment Plant ($19.9M contract), Roads & Drainage improvements, six modular housing units, and Chimanah Wellness Centre. COVID-19 noted as significant event with uncertain future impact.
notes: Audit was qualified due to lack of sufficient audit evidence for Ahousaht Fishing Corporation (financial information not available at audit report date). Major one-time item: $4,917,699 forgiveness of Treaty loan (land claim negotiation debt). Also includes $47,373 forgiveness of CMHC loans and $1,100,000 distribution to 1197013 B.C. Ltd. Consolidated entities include Ahousaht Education Authority, 1139763 B.C. Ltd., Cha Chum Hi Yup Tiic Miss Society, and Chimanah Wellness Centre - 1197013 B.C. Ltd. Ahousaht Fishing Corporation accounted for by modified equity method. Budget was not formally approved by Council, which is a departure from PSA standards. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($21,470,951) + NTC ($3,642,396) + First Nations School Association ($1,239,258) + Province of BC ($449,549) + Aboriginal Head Start ($181,518) + CMHC ($84,316) + First Nation Health Authority ($223,063) = $27,291,051. OSR includes other revenue, rental income, forestry, gaming, interest, internet services.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
